description: Fix iPhone photo upload timeouts and add HEIC/HEIF support — early downscale in anime filter, accept iOS image formats, increase client timeout, remove disruptive avatar overlay icon

userStories.title: Early downscale in anime filter pipeline
userStories.description: As an iPhone user, I want my photo to process quickly so that the upload doesn't time out. The anime filter in services/api/src/lib/anime-filter.ts currently processes at the original image resolution (up to 48MP for iPhone photos) before resizing to 256×256 at the very end. This must be changed to downscale first.
acceptanceCriteria: In anime-filter.ts, after reading metadata and before the posterize/edge-detection pipeline, resize the input to a max of 1024px on the longest side (maintain aspect ratio) so all subsequent steps operate on the smaller image
The three parallel sharp operations (posterize, edgesX, edgesY) use the downscaled dimensions instead of the original width/height
Output is still 256×256 WebP (unchanged)
EXIF auto-rotation is still applied via sharp().rotate()
All existing anime filter tests in anime-filter.test.ts still pass
Add a test that creates a large 4032×3024 JPEG input and verifies the anime filter completes in under 10 seconds
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The key change is in anime-filter.ts lines 36-38 where width/height are read from metadata and used as-is. Instead, cap both to 1024px max while preserving aspect ratio. The final resize to OUTPUT_SIZE (256) at the end remains unchanged.

userStories.title: Accept HEIC/HEIF on the server
userStories.description: As an iOS user, I want to upload photos in HEIC format so I don't get 'Invalid file type' errors. The ALLOWED_IMAGE_TYPES array in services/api/src/routes/users.ts must include HEIC/HEIF MIME types.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'image/heic' and 'image/heif' to the ALLOWED_IMAGE_TYPES array in services/api/src/routes/users.ts
The error message for invalid file types does NOT need to list HEIC (keep it generic or add HEIC to the list)
Add a unit test in users-profile-picture.test.ts that uploads a file with mimetype 'image/heic' and verifies it is accepted (not rejected with 400)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sharp 0.34.5 bundles libvips with HEIF decode support in its prebuilt binaries — no extra system packages needed. The test can use a small JPEG buffer with the mimetype header set to image/heic since multer checks mimetype not actual bytes.

userStories.title: Accept HEIC/HEIF in web file picker
userStories.description: As an iPhone user using Safari, I want the file picker to allow me to select HEIC photos directly instead of relying on Safari's inconsistent auto-conversion.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/web/src/app/(main)/account/page.tsx, update the ACCEPTED_IMAGE_TYPES constant (currently 'image/jpeg,image/png,image/webp') to also include 'image/heic,image/heif'
The hidden file input's accept attribute automatically picks up the new value since it uses the constant
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The constant is defined near the top of the file (line 15). Just append ',image/heic,image/heif' to the string.

userStories.title: Increase client upload timeout to 45 seconds
userStories.description: As a mobile user on a slower connection, I want a more generous upload timeout so legitimate uploads don't get cut off by the 20-second client-side abort.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/web/src/lib/api.ts, change the timeoutMs constant in apiUploadFile from 20_000 to 45_000
The timeout error message remains unchanged: 'Upload timed out. Please try again with a smaller image.'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The timeout is on line 73 of api.ts: const timeoutMs = 20_000. Change to 45_000. The server-side anime filter timeout is 15s, so 45s gives ample room for network transfer on slow mobile connections.

userStories.title: Remove disruptive camera icon overlay on avatar
userStories.description: As a user, I don't want a white square obscuring my profile picture. Remove the camera SVG icon overlay from the avatar button on the account page.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the <span> element containing the camera SVG icon and its hover overlay from the avatar button in apps/web/src/app/(main)/account/page.tsx (the span with classes 'absolute inset-0 flex items-center...' and the inline camera SVG)
The avatar button remains clickable and still opens the file picker on click
The focus ring styling for accessibility is preserved (focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary focus:ring-offset-2)
Add a subtle hover effect on the button: group-hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity for a gentle visual cue
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The overlay span is inside the avatar button, after the Avatar component render. Remove the entire {!uploadPictureMutation.isPending && (...)} block. Add opacity transition classes to the button itself.
